mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and strong economic activity, which typically supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month average growth is consistently positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 55% of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to investor pessimism and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by the specified trigger, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is simultaneously rising, it indicates aggressive monetary tightening to combat overheating, which increases the cost of capital and negatively impacts equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If the specified inflation and rate conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 35% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained recovery in housing starts (HOUST) after a downturn indicates renewed consumer confidence and investment in real estate, which typically precedes broader economic recovery and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average is above its 12-month moving average by more than 5%.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows the specified recovery, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such periods within a declared recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and consistent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer spending, a key driver of corporate revenues and profits, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS 3-month annualized growth is consistently above 3% but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates increasing manufacturing output and capacity utilization, signaling a healthy supply side of the economy that supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth meets the trigger condition, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces borrowing costs for companies and encourages investment, thereby supporting equity valuations and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% for 3 consecutive months.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is slowing while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is at historically low levels, it suggests a late-cycle environment where growth is decelerating but labor costs may be rising, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 1-year growth rate is below 2% AND UNRATE is below 5%.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth slows below 2% while UNRATE is below 5%, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 30% of triggered instances.
